summary: Cabinly Iteration 2 testing complete. Backend: 34/34 pytest passing (20 regression + 14 new for AC/Non-AC type, mock UPI boost, reviews with past-date guard, chat threads with auto-welcome, participant-only 403s, cabin_type/owner_id/can_review/has_review on GET /bookings/{id}). Frontend (Expo web 390x844) verified end-to-end: student signup -> AC/Non-AC chip row (18 total, 7 Non-AC, filter works), 5 featured badges visible on home, cabin detail shows type chip + Featured chip + avg_rating 4.0(1) + Reviews list; booking creates and appears on Bookings tab with Chat + Cancel buttons + AC type pill + CONFIRMED status; chat screen opens with auto-welcome message from owner, send works. Owner (demo.owner@cabinly.app) verified: My Cabins each with Boost button, Boost modal shows 'cabinly@upi' + Confirm Paid -> Featured badge appears on card + 'Boosted' pill on button; Add Cabin has segmented AC/Non-AC control (defaults AC), Non-AC choice persists in listing. No red screens, no page errors, no deprecated Expo packages, all interactive elements have testIDs. No functional bugs found.

issues: Confirm-booking-button can fall below fold on shorter viewports; users must scroll inside modal. Consider ScrollView with sticky footer or shorter modal spacing.
Confirm-paid-button is outside the initial viewport at 390x844 with the QR image + address block; requires scrolling. Consider making the modal body scrollable or reducing QR size on small screens.

action_items: (Optional UX) Make Book-now and Boost modals scrollable so their primary CTA is always reachable on 390x667.
(Optional, from iter1) FastAPI on_event startup/shutdown is deprecated; migrate to lifespan handlers.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py enrich_cabin recomputes stats via 1 aggregate per cabin per list call — fine at current scale, but consider a $lookup pipeline if the catalog grows.
server.py _cabin_is_featured tolerates both string and datetime featured_until values; good defensive parsing.
index.tsx: student sees type chip row unconditionally; owner sees My Cabins + Boost buttons. Clean separation via role gate.
bookings.tsx: 'Leave a review' button only rendered when can_review is true — matches backend contract exactly.
chat/[bookingId].tsx: 3s polling implemented; consider stopping the interval when the tab is blurred to save network.
seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_ / it2_ prefixed users, cabins, bookings, and 1 review created via public API and one past-dated booking inserted via motor (test_success_path_past_date_via_mongo). All isolated from seeded demo owner data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Notes: (1) EmailStr rejects .test TLD -> use .app; (2) tab-* and cabin-list testIDs render twice in Expo web (top+bottom bars) — use `.last` when clicking; (3) Book-now / Boost modal CTAs can fall below fold at 390x844, use dispatch_event('click') or evaluate() to bypass viewport check; (4) featured_until returned as ISO datetime string with tz; compare with datetime.fromisoformat(...replace('Z','+00:00')); (5) past-date review success path requires inserting the booking directly via motor to bypass frontend date-picker (past dates are not selectable) — pattern shown in test_iteration2_features.py::test_success_path_past_date_via_mongo.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. All new features (AC/Non-AC type + filter, mock UPI featured boost, past-date-only reviews with avg_rating rollup, per-booking chat with auto-welcome + participant-only access) function as specified in the review request.
